name: repomix
description: packs an entire repository into a single AI-friendly context file

developer-org.comment: Created and maintained by individual developer yamadashy (Kazuki Yamada) with community contributions.

implementation-language.comment: Built with TypeScript on Node.js; distributed as an npm package.

first-release.comment: Originally announced as 'Repopack' on 2024-07-16; renamed to 'Repomix' on 2024-12-01 for legal reasons.

status.comment: Actively developed; latest release v1.16.0 dated 2026-06-29 (checked 2026-07-09).

form-factor.comment: Primarily a CLI; also usable as a Node.js library, ships an MCP server (--mcp), and a hosted web version at repomix.com. A community-maintained VS Code extension also exists.

standalone-usable.comment: Standalone tool runnable from any toolchain; not welded into a single host agent.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone tool — not bundled inside a host agent/IDE.

install-mechanism.comment: Install via npm/npx (also yarn, bun, Homebrew); Docker image at ghcr.io/yamadashy/repomix; community VS Code editor extension; and a hosted browser version at repomix.com.

retrieval-technique.comment: Serializes the whole repository (or a glob-filtered slice) into one output blob with no query-time selection. Optional --compress uses Tree-sitter to reduce code to function/class signatures, but it is still whole-repo packing, not query-driven retrieval.

query-interface.comment: The core packer takes no query (none). Its MCP server exposes tool calls (pack_codebase, pack_remote_repository) and a grep_repomix_output tool for regex/lexical search over the packed output.

returns.comment: Emits a single packed repository blob (XML/Markdown/JSON/plain text) that includes a directory-structure file list plus concatenated file contents.

structural-awareness.comment: Default packing is plain-text concatenation, but the opt-in --compress mode uses Tree-sitter to extract and preserve function/class/interface signatures — genuine AST parsing.

chunking-strategy.comment: Whole files are packed as units. With --compress, Tree-sitter reduces each file to symbol signatures (effectively symbol-level), but no separate index chunking occurs.

reranking.comment: No retrieval/ranking stage — it packs, it does not rank.

index-location.comment: Stateless packer — builds no persistent index; regenerates the output on each run.

incremental-updates.comment: No index to update — each invocation re-reads and re-packs the repository from scratch.

vector-store.comment: No embeddings, so no vector store.

embedding-model.comment: n-a — repomix does not use embeddings.

mcp-support.comment: Ships an MCP server (repomix --mcp) exposing pack/read/grep tools.

agent-compatibility.comment: Generic MCP server usable by any MCP-capable agent; docs call out Claude and Cursor. Output file can also be pasted into any LLM chat (ChatGPT, Gemini, etc.).

ide-integrations.comment: Primarily a terminal CLI; community-maintained VS Code extension; hosted web app at repomix.com.

language-agnostic.comment: Default packing works on any text-based files regardless of language. The optional --compress and comment-removal features are limited to Tree-sitter-supported languages.

supported-languages.value: any (text-based) for packing; Tree-sitter compression/comment-removal supports ~15+ languages (JS/TS, Python, Java, Go, Rust, C/C++, Ruby, PHP, and more)

code-leaves-machine.comment: The CLI/library runs fully locally; no source is sent anywhere by repomix itself (the user then feeds the output to whatever LLM they choose). The optional hosted repomix.com web version is a separate opt-in path.

local-only-option.comment: Runs entirely offline; no external API required.

self-hostable.comment: OSS you run locally; the web version can also be self-hosted from the repo (website/ app).

telemetry.comment: No telemetry documented in the CLI/library (checked 2026-07-09).

max-repo-scale.comment: Can process large repos, but the practical ceiling is the target LLM's context window since it packs everything into one blob; glob filtering and --compress extend usable scale. Not monorepo-scale retrieval infrastructure.

pricing-model.comment: Free and open source; hosted web version is also free.

maturity.comment: Past v1.0 (v1.16.0), widely adopted (~27k stars), stable and polished, still actively iterating.

active-maintenance.comment: v1.16.0 released 2026-06-29, well within 90 days of the research date (checked 2026-07-09).

community-size.comment: RESEARCH.md anchors repomix as large; ~27k GitHub stars and broad ecosystem (Homebrew, Docker, browser extensions) support this.
